title: Assign weight by color in Status and Progress columns
excerpt:
As a user, I would appreciate a higher degree of granularity/depth between the Status and Progress columns. 
I believe it would be a great thing to allow weight distribution by status color/label and have the progress co…

posts.author: Danny (DIGITAL STEAM)
posts.cookedHtml:
As a user, I would appreciate a higher degree of granularity/depth between the Status and Progress columns.

I believe it would be a great thing to allow weight distribution by status color/label and have the progress column calculate it’s own weight based on these instead of a 0%/100% limitation just on the done status.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Do you mean something similar to what is explained here?

Support

The Progress Tracking Column

1 min read Feature   The progress tracking column allows you to combine all the status columns of each item into one, beautiful battery-like view. This column is a must to visually keep track o...

Or something different?

posts.author: StephaneGout (Stephane Gout)
posts.cookedHtml:
The idea -if I understood right- is to chose each status color weight.

For example, green is 100%, yellow is 75%, orange is 50%…

So that if you have 2 status columns, not only the weight of the column is calculated (like the new feature), but also the weight of each selected status.

For example, if I have 2 status column :

  • 1st column makes up to 40%
  • 2nd column makes up to 60%

So when the 1st column is orange and we chose orange weights 50%, the 2nd is red that weights 0%, the progress bar will display : 40%*50% + 0% = 20%

If we can’t chose the weight of each status, it would have displayed 0 or 40%.

I hope my explanation is clear

posts.author: Danny (DIGITAL STEAM)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yup, that’s correct Stephane
Thanks
Danny

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: Interesting feedback and I see how this could be helpful. We’ll pass this along for consideration!

posts.author: Thao.SFE (Thao T. )
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree with this! We use a “SHIP” or “WILL CALL” status column. Each one of these contributes to a small percentage of completion status. It would be nice to be able to break it down and set the percentage weight for each one.

My example:
Need label
Label made
Picked Up
En route

posts.author: viviannaidoo (Vivian Naidoo)
posts.cookedHtml:
I also need this feature. Made my own post for it as well

Set custom Status Bar completion percent Feature requests
Hi there, I recently signed up for Monday.com and I’ve been enjoying it as I can view the progress of all my projects at a glance on the timeline. I’m currently using it to track the progress of my video production pipeline from the filming to editing stages by using a status column linked to a due date for each of the main tasks and a progress tracker bar to calculate the total progress of all tasks. My problem comes in when I have to track a single task which is lengthy in duration for exam…

posts.author: wyrobot (peter)
posts.cookedHtml:
We need the same. We have 3 stage status columns - Planning, Developing and Deploying. Each of those columns has the following statuses: Initiated, 50% complete, 75% complete, 100% Complete.

If the weight of the stages are: Planning 25%, Developing 50%, Deploying 25%.
It’s more meaningful to us if we also factor in the progress of each column.

Planning Stage = 100% Complete = 1.0 x 25% = 25%
Developing Stage = 50% Complete = 0.5 x 50% = 25%
Total Real progress = 50% Complete

The progress bar only calculates the first column and the overall project as 25%, but from our perspective, it is 50% compete

https://d.pr/i/JixZFY

posts.author: DeedyA (Deedy Alvarez-Sample)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be nice to also have the option to set Progress by singular status column values for simple tracking scenarios:

Initiated = 25%
Executing = 50%
Testing = 75%
Done = 100%

posts.author: Chris_M (Chris McCulloch)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey guys, if you open the “Column settings” you can set the weighting of each status column.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Chris_M:

if you open the “Column settings” you can set the weighting of each status column.

Understood. However, this request wants to weight the color in each Status as well.

e.g. Planning 25% x 34%, Development 20% x 33%, Delivery 10% x 33% = Progress %

As it stands now, we have 100% (Done) or 0% (all other colors) for each Status column x weight = Progress %

posts.author: pep_pericas (Pep Pericàs)
posts.cookedHtml: I have same requirement

posts.author: AlejandraGelfi (Alejandra Gelfi)
posts.cookedHtml: Me too! It would be great if we could choose a label colour with no weight in the progress of the pulse. This is mandatory for the correct progress percentage if a pulse does not require one or more stages to be fulfilled.

posts.author: rdeelen (Rohan)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi all, stumbled on this thread, and I would like like to see the same feature implemented.

posts.author: nikolamujdzic (Nikola Mujdzic Rescic)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes i have a process with three status column. If I put all three columns I can distribute equally each status in column 1 column 2 and 3.

I need to set percentage in column 1 depending on a status, status 1 is 15%, status 2 is 0%, status 3 i 20%, and same in other two columns.

posts.author: BenWilkinson (Ben Wilkinson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would love to see this implemented as well.
Additionally, is there a way to connect subitem status to item progress as well?

posts.author: guillemarqueta (Guillermo Marqueta)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Julia-monday.com any news on this?

posts.author: itaishabtai (itai shabtai)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everybody!

Itai here from monday.com team. First of all, I want to thank you all for the great feedback - this is truly an amazing idea to have and I will make sure to note it again to our team. That being said, I do want to be transparent and let you know we have to prioritise our tasks according to scope, effort and the roadmap ahead, and I can’t see this being implement it soon.

Moving forward, I’d love to offer a workaround for anyone who wants to visualise their workflow per label. With the combination of the Formula column and conditional coloring, you can set an IF() formula to show different percentage and color the value accordingly. This will require either a SWITCH function or an IF function, and you can learn more about how to use it here:
The IF Function

Basically, you’ll set the labels as conditions, e.g: If label is X then 25%, if Y then 50% and use the SUM (or a simple plus sign) to aggregate the information.

The conditional coloring can be set to color the values if reach to specific value, and help you visualise it better. Does this make sense?

Let us know if you have any more insights!
Itai

posts.author: DrewLeFever (Drew LeFever)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Itai, I’ve set up an IF statement to calculate each status as a percentage - but the Progress column cannot connect to that Formula column. Is there something I’m doing wrong? Maybe I followed your instructions wrong. What are you referring to to with a SUM function?

posts.author: AndrewL (Andrew L)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was hoping for this too!

I just want to clarify, it may not be needed but that’s ok. This is how I was hoping it’d work.

ex. In one status column, there are 4 status’. (grey, red, yellow, green.) each status has it’s own weight that’s applied to the progress bar. grey is 10, red is 30, yellow is 60 and green is 100.

So instead of having however many colors in one column that all mark “complete”, it would be a more [granular] percentage that goes to the progress bar.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can use native conditional coloring based off formula columns to achieve this.

Thanks,
Mark

posts.author: AndrewL (Andrew L)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ll look into that, but if it involves coding, do you have a video for that?
